qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary command execution via DD_TELEMETRY_FORWARDER_PATH environment variable without validation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets DD_TELEMETRY_FORWARDER_PATH flowing to ProcessBuilder during agent telemetry bootstrap, while the advisory targets RMI/JMX instrumentation deserializing incoming data without serialization filters.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described RMI/JMX unsafe deserialization endpoint or the missing serialization-filter root cause.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-579q-h82j-r5v2 describes dd-trace-java RMI instrumentation registering a custom endpoint that deserializes incoming data without serialization filters, exploitable over a network-reachable JMX/RMI port on JDK 16 or earlier with a gadget-chain-compatible classpath.
The only final qualifying finding describes the DD_TELEMETRY_FORWARDER_PATH environment variable being passed to ProcessBuilder during Java agent telemetry bootstrap.

The finding's reported code locations are AgentBootstrap.java, BootstrapInitializationTelemetry.java, and AgentPreCheck.java, not an RMI/JMX deserialization endpoint or serialization filter bypass path.
